name: spokenly
title: Spokenly
description: Control Spokenly speech-to-text: dictation, transcription history, file transcription, model selection, and quick commands
author: mattiacolombomc

commands.name: copy-last-transcript
commands.title: Copy Last Transcript
commands.subtitle: Spokenly
commands.description: Copy the most recent transcription to clipboard

commands.name: paste-last-transcript
commands.title: Paste Last Transcript
commands.subtitle: Spokenly
commands.description: Paste the most recent transcription into the active app

commands.name: search-transcripts
commands.title: Search Transcripts
commands.subtitle: Spokenly
commands.description: Browse, copy, reveal and delete transcription history

commands.name: transcribe-file
commands.title: Transcribe File
commands.subtitle: Spokenly
commands.description: Transcribe an audio or video file through Spokenly

commands.name: open-history-folder
commands.title: Open History Folder
commands.subtitle: Spokenly
commands.description: Reveal the Spokenly history directory in Finder

commands.name: open-last-recording
commands.title: Open Last Recording
commands.subtitle: Spokenly
commands.description: Reveal the most recent recording in Finder

commands.name: select-model
commands.title: Select Dictation Model
commands.subtitle: Spokenly
commands.description: Switch the active dictation transcription model

commands.name: select-file-model
commands.title: Select File Transcription Model
commands.subtitle: Spokenly
commands.description: Switch the model used to transcribe files

commands.name: run-quick-command
commands.title: Run Quick Command
commands.subtitle: Spokenly
commands.description: Trigger one of your Spokenly quick commands

preferences.name: mcpPort
preferences.title: MCP Server Port
preferences.description: Port where Spokenly's MCP server listens (default 51089)

preferences.name: useShortcutFallback
preferences.title: Toggle Dictation Fallback
preferences.label: Simulate the global shortcut instead of using the URL scheme
preferences.description: Use System Events to replay your Spokenly dictation shortcut. Required if the URL scheme cannot stop an in-progress dictation. Needs Accessibility permission for Raycast.
